articles.title: Teaching AI to run with the turbines
articles.summary:
Teaching AI to run with the turbines
    [Summary]
    https://www.technologyreview.com/2026/07/02/1138433/teaching-ai-to-run-with-the-turbines/</thought>## ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE

Teaching AI to run with the turbines
The article examines the strategic pivot of artificial intelligence from consumer-facing generative tools toward the critical operational layers of industrial infrastructure. By integrating AI into high-stakes environments—such as turbine management and energy systems—the focus shifts from creative output to operational continuity, safety, and systemic reliability. This transition toward "industrial AI" highlights a move where the technology acts as a core operating layer for physical assets, prioritizing predictive maintenance and risk mitigation over simple automation. The analysis suggests that the most consequential AI advancements may occur invisibly within the backbone of global infrastructure, where the cost of failure is high and the requirement for precision is absolute, marking a maturation of AI from a novelty to an essential industrial utility.
https://www.technologyreview.com/2026/07/02/1138433/teaching-ai-to-run-with-the-turbines/

Why California’s carbon manure math doesn’t add up
articles.summary:
Why California’s carbon manure math doesn’t add up
    [Summary]
    [URL]</thought>## POLICY & REGULATION

Why California’s carbon manure math doesn’t add up
California's initiative to incentivize cattle farmers to convert methane emissions from manure into renewable natural gas (RNG) is facing critical scrutiny over its actual environmental efficacy. While the program is designed to mitigate potent greenhouse gases, evidence suggests the underlying carbon accounting is flawed, potentially rewarding farmers for emissions that would have occurred regardless or failing to account for the full lifecycle of the gas produced. This discrepancy highlights a systemic vulnerability in climate policy: the reliance on complex credit mechanisms that may prioritize industrial output over genuine atmospheric carbon reduction. The situation underscores the tension between creating economically viable transitions for the agricultural sector and achieving rigorous, verifiable climate goals, signaling a need for more transparent and scientifically grounded regulatory frameworks.
https://www.technologyreview.com/2026/07/02/1139981/why-californias-carbon-manure-math-doesnt-add-up/

Inside Genebench-Pro
Introducing GeneBench-Pro
articles.summary:
Introducing GeneBench-Pro
    OpenAI has introduced GeneBench-Pro, a specialized benchmark designed to evaluate the capabilities of artificial intelligence within the fields of genomics, biology, and broader scientific research. By utilizing complex, real-world datasets, GeneBench-Pro aims to move beyond synthetic tests to provide a more rigorous assessment of how AI models handle the intricacies of biological data. This development is significant as it establishes a standardized metric for measuring progress in AI-driven scientific discovery, potentially accelerating breakthroughs in drug discovery and personalized medicine. The initiative underscores a strategic shift toward integrating frontier AI models into specialized scientific domains, ensuring that model performance is validated against the high precision requirements of the life sciences.
    https://openai.com/index/introducing-genebench-pro

Constructive Alignment: Governing Preference Dynamics in Human-AI Interaction
articles.summary:
Constructive Alignment: Governing Preference Dynamics in Human-AI Interaction
    This paper introduces "Constructive Alignment," a new paradigm for AI alignment that challenges the traditional view of human preferences as static targets. Instead, the authors argue that preferences are dynamic and shaped by interaction with AI systems. By utilizing a control-theoretic framework grounded in behavioral economics and psychology, the research proposes that alignment should focus on governing the trajectory of human value formation. The goal is to ensure that AI-driven preference evolution remains epistemically grounded and resistant to manipulation. This shift moves the alignment problem from simple preference satisfaction to the active regulation of how AI influences human evaluative states over time, providing a more sophisticated approach to long-term human-AI coexistence.
    https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.00001</thought>## RESEARCH & ACADEMIC BREAKTHROUGHS

Constructive Alignment: Governing Preference Dynamics in Human-AI Interaction
This paper introduces "Constructive Alignment," a new paradigm for AI alignment that challenges the traditional view of human preferences as static targets to be optimized. Instead, the authors argue that preferences are dynamic and actively shaped by interaction with AI systems. By utilizing a control-theoretic framework grounded in behavioral economics and psychology, the research proposes that alignment should focus on governing the trajectory of human value formation rather than simple preference satisfaction. The goal is to ensure that AI-driven preference evolution remains epistemically grounded, reflectively endorsed, and resistant to manipulation. This shift moves the alignment problem toward the active regulation of how AI influences human evaluative states over time, providing a more sophisticated approach to long-term human-AI coexistence.
https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.00001

articles.title: Bounded Morality: Defining the Space of Moral Computation
articles.summary:
Bounded Morality: Defining the Space of Moral Computation
    [Summary]
    https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.00002</thought>## RESEARCH & ACADEMIC BREAKTHROUGHS

Bounded Morality: Defining the Space of Moral Computation
This paper introduces "Bounded Morality," a formal framework that applies the concept of bounded rationality to moral cognition. By defining moral situations through "moral breadth"—the scope of relevant entities—and "moral depth"—the complexity of inferential integration—the authors argue that finite agents face inherent computational tradeoffs. Rather than viewing ethical theories as absolute truths, the framework positions them as locally efficient strategies optimized for specific resource constraints. Crucially, the research suggests a paradigm shift for AI alignment: instead of focusing on the imitation of human moral judgments, developers should prioritize the scaling and strategic allocation of an agent's moral reasoning capacity. This approach provides a mathematical basis for understanding moral regret and progress within the constraints of finite computation.
https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.00002
